crons.name: heartbeat
crons.prompt: Read HEARTBEAT.md and follow its instructions. Update your heartbeat, check inbox, and work on your highest priority task.

crons.name: usage-monitor
crons.prompt: Check Claude API usage: run officeos bus check-usage-api --warn-7day 80 --warn-5h 90 2>&1. Parse the JSON output. Rules: (1) If 7-day utilization >= 80% send the orchestrator a CODE RED via Telegram immediately. (2) If 5-hour >= 90% send a warning. (3) For 7-day: check if the current utilization has crossed a new 10% increment since the last check (e.g. crossed 10%, 20%, 30%... 90%). Read the last logged 7-day value from today's memory file to compare. If a new 10% threshold was crossed, send the orchestrator a brief Telegram: '7-day usage: X% (resets <date>)'. Same logic applies to codex.utilization_5h and codex.utilization_7d fields. (4) Always log the current levels in your daily memory.

crons.name: nightly-metrics
crons.prompt: Run nightly metrics collection: officeos bus collect-metrics. Review the output and report any anomalies via Telegram to the orchestrator.

crons.name: auto-commit
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/local-version-control/SKILL.md and execute the daily version control workflow. Stage, review, and commit changes.

crons.name: check-upstream
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/upstream-sync/SKILL.md and execute the upstream sync workflow. Check for framework updates and report to the orchestrator.

crons.name: catalog-browse
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/catalog-browse/SKILL.md and execute the catalog browse workflow. Check for new community skills and recommend useful ones.

crons.name: jd-improvement-review
crons.prompt: Read interaction logs for all agents: look for orgs/*/agents/*/logs/interactions.jsonl files. Find patterns: what was escalated most? What triggered ASK_HUMAN most? What queries had no matching agent? Propose specific JD updates (new keywords, responsibilities, provides/needs changes) per agent. Write proposals to the org jd-proposals.md for human review. Also recommend autonomy expansions: which agents have high handle-rate on categories currently always escalated?
